text.title: Agent Kill-Switch Design Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of agent-kill-switch-design enforces. Each rule has a statement, a rationale anchored to industry practice, and a source citation.

rule.statement: Kill switch has 3 scopes: global (all tenants), per-tenant, per-tool. Each scope is independently togglable.
rule.rationale: Single-scope kill is too blunt for production; 3 scopes match the actual failure shapes (provider outage, single-tenant abuse, single-tool bug).

rule.statement: End-to-end kill latency (toggle → no new requests reach LLM) is ≤5 seconds at p95.
rule.rationale: Beyond 5 seconds the kill is theatre; per-request cache lookups must propagate within that window.

rule.statement: Kill switch is tested in production-equivalent staging on day 0 AND retested at every release.
rule.rationale: Untested kill switches fail when needed; per-release retest catches wiring regressions.

rule.statement: Recovery path is documented and tested: who flips back, under what condition, with which approval.
rule.rationale: Without a recovery path the kill becomes a permanent outage; explicit path prevents over-cautious freeze.

rule.statement: Every kill event emits a structured log + alert: scope, actor, reason, timestamp; consumed by incident channel.
rule.rationale: Silent kills bypass the team; structured events make root cause and recovery traceable.

rule.statement: Stub rule for conclusion 'skip-methodology' referenced from 06-decision-tree.xml. Replace with the real testable rule that resolves this branch of the tree.
rule.rationale: Auto-added by fix-methodology-phase-d.py to keep tree refs resolvable.
